config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society’s interests should automatically come before individual rights, because rights are what protect people from being sacrificed for vague notions of the “greater good.” In my view, a society is healthier when it respects personal freedom, due process, and dignity, even while balancing them with public needs like safety and order. From my perspective, once society is given too much priority, it can easily become an excuse for abuse or authoritarianism.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu justru menjadi pagar utama agar negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” dan sejarah kita penuh contoh ketika alasan itu dipakai untuk membenarkan diskriminasi atau penyalahgunaan kuasa. Tentu masyarakat butuh aturan, keamanan, dan tanggung jawab bersama, tetapi prioritas itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari perlakuan sewenang-wenang. Kalau hak individu selalu bisa dikorbankan demi kepentingan kolektif yang kabur, maka yang tersisa bukan masyarakat yang sehat, melainkan masyarakat yang mudah menindas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Individual rights should not be treated as secondary to society’s interests, because once the collective is given automatic priority, it becomes too easy to justify abuse in the name of order or unity. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to “the common good” can sometimes be used to silence dissent or pressure people to conform, so I am wary of any argument that makes individual rights conditional by default. Society does matter, of course, but its interests should be balanced against rights, not placed above them. A healthy community protects people first, because a society that can freely sacrifice individuals is not really healthy at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; menurut saya hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, bukan dikalahkan begitu saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas kolektif yang terlalu luas. Kepentingan publik memang penting untuk keamanan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban, tetapi justru harus dijalankan dengan hormat pada kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang. Kalau hak bisa dikorbankan setiap kali mayoritas merasa itu berguna, maka yang dilindungi bukan masyarakat, melainkan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I am very cautious about any idea that lets the collective override personal rights too easily, because “for the common good” can become a convenient excuse for pressure, conformity, and abuse of power. Society does need rules and shared responsibility, but those should exist to protect people, not to make individuals disposable. A healthy society is one where public interests are pursued within firm limits set by basic rights and dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau perluasan kuasa negara yang seharusnya dibatasi. Memang ada situasi darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi bahkan di situ hak dasar tidak boleh dianggap bisa dicabut sesuka mayoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling disiplin melindungi hak setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
